response_text:
To find out how many telephone calls Jason needs to make, we can work backward from his sales goal using the ratios provided:

1.  **Determine how many customers need to visit the dealership:**
    The ratio of customers to car sales is 2:1 (two customers for every one car sold).
    $15 \text{ cars} \times 2 \text{ customers/car} = 30 \text{ customers needed.}$

2.  **Determine how many telephone calls are needed to get those customers:**
    The ratio of telephone calls to customers is 25:1 (25 calls for every one customer).
    $30 \text{ customers} \times 25 \text{ calls/customer} = 750 \text{ calls.}$

**Jason needs to make 750 telephone calls to sell 15 cars.**
